question: Develop a C++ program that prompts the user to enter their first name (as a string), age (as an integer), and height in meters (as a double). After receiving all inputs, print a formatted sentence summarizing this information (e.g., 'Hello [Name], you are [Age] years old and [Height] meters tall.').

test_cases.expected: Enter your first name: Enter your age: Enter your height in meters: Hello Alice, you are 30 years old and 1.75 meters tall.
Enter your first name: Enter your age: Enter your height in meters: Hello Bob, you are 0 years old and 0 meters tall.
Enter your first name: Enter your age: Enter your height in meters: Hello Charlie, you are 25 years old and 0 meters tall.

errors: The program lacks input validation for numeric types (age and height). If the user enters non-numeric input, `std::cin` enters a fail state, and the variables (age, height) may retain their default initialized values (0) or contain garbage, leading to incorrect output without any error message or retry mechanism. Subsequent `std::cin` operations might also fail or behave unexpectedly.
